name: History of Tamil Society and Related Archaeological Discoveries

sourceRefs.label: 2025_q page 75 Q79 (Arikkamedu/Chera-Chola/Sangam-Age statements, with answer key)
2022_q1 page 23 Q131-132 (Velaan Vedham; Wheeler's stratigraphy method at Arikkamedu, with answer key)
2026_q page 8 Q7 (Keezhadi pottery/Tamizhi script literacy evidence, with answer key)

researchNotes: Opens Part A Unit VI (History, Culture, Heritage, and Socio-Political Movements in Tamil Nadu, 20 questions — tied with Unit V as the largest GS unit). 4 real hits, all clustering on the two most actively-discussed TN excavation sites — Arikkamedu and Keezhadi — both of which carry real archaeological/political significance for dating Tamil civilization's antiquity. Cross-references Day 1's fact about Wheeler's stratigraphy method (already used there for the General Science 'science as a tool to understand the past' angle) from the History angle here.

reading.intro: Covers two of Tamil Nadu's most significant excavation sites — Arikkamedu (a Roman-era trade port) and Keezhadi (early literacy evidence) — plus a few core Sangam-era political facts. Real exam evidence favours testing exact site-to-finding pairings and common Chera/Chola mix-ups.

qa.question: Real exam: What evidence has been found at Arikkamedu, and which excavation method was used there?
qa.answer: Evidence of a planned town, warehouses, and ring wells has been found at Arikkamedu (near Puducherry) — confirmed by a real exam question. Sir Mortimer Wheeler excavated Arikkamedu using the scientific stratigraphy (soil-layer) method — also confirmed by a separate real exam question.

qa.question: Real exam: Who actually ruled the Kaveri delta from Uraiyur — and what's the common mix-up?
qa.answer: The CHOLAS ruled the Kaveri delta, with their capital at Uraiyur — a real exam flagged 'The Cheras ruled over the Kaveri delta with capital Uraiyur' as FALSE; the Cheras actually ruled the Kerala region (capital at Vanji/Karur), a different dynasty entirely from the Cholas.

qa.question: Real exam: What was the Chola royal emblem, and what did their coins feature?
qa.answer: The Chola emblem was the TIGER. They issued square copper coins bearing images of the tiger — confirmed by a real exam question.

qa.question: Real exam: Does the Sangam Age have its roots in the Bronze Age?
qa.answer: NO — a real exam flagged this claim as FALSE. The Sangam Age is generally associated with the Iron Age / early historic period (roughly 300 BCE-300 CE), not the earlier Bronze Age.

qa.question: Real exam: What evidence from the Keezhadi (Keeladi) excavation suggests people in 6th century BC were literate?
qa.answer: Pots found at Keezhadi bear Tamizhi script engraved on them, AND most of these pots were inscribed with letters AFTER the pot had already dried (not before firing) — both confirmed true by a real exam question, together forming the literacy evidence.

qa.question: Real exam: Which Tamil moral/wisdom text is also referred to as 'Velaan Vedham'?
qa.answer: Proverbs (the collected Tamil proverbs) — confirmed by a real exam question, distinguishing it from Thirukkural, Aarakkovai, and Naladiyaar offered as distractors.

questions.prompt: Evidence of a planned town, warehouses, and ring wells has been found at which excavation site?
Sir Mortimer Wheeler used which excavation method/technique at Arikkamedu?
Which dynasty actually ruled the Kaveri delta, with their capital at Uraiyur?
What was the Chola royal emblem, featured on their square copper coins?
The Sangam Age is generally dated to which broad period?
What script is engraved on pots found at the Keezhadi excavation site?
At Keezhadi, most pots were inscribed with letters:
options: While the clay was still wet on the wheel
